resources.title: NI USRP 2921
resources.subtitle: Software-defined radio transceiver for wireless research
resources.description: The NI USRP 2921 is a tunable RF transceiver designed for prototyping and deploying wireless communication systems. It integrates seamlessly with NI LabVIEW for rapid signal processing development.
resources.overview: The NI USRP 2921 operates from 2.4 GHz to 2.5 GHz and 4.9 GHz to 5.9 GHz, providing a versatile platform for teaching and researching wireless communication protocols including Wi-Fi, LTE, and custom OFDM waveforms. In Shannon Lab, it is used for hands-on experiments in digital communication, channel estimation, and MIMO systems. Paired with LabVIEW, students can design, simulate and deploy real wireless systems on actual RF hardware.

resources.title: NI USRP 2922
resources.subtitle: Wideband software-defined radio for advanced research
resources.description: The NI USRP 2922 provides a wider frequency range from 400 MHz to 4.4 GHz, enabling research across multiple wireless bands and protocols.
resources.overview: The NI USRP 2922 extends the frequency coverage to 400 MHz–4.4 GHz, making it ideal for exploring cellular, ISM band, and public safety communication standards. It supports real-time streaming of I/Q data and works with LabVIEW Communications Design Suite for rapid prototyping of next-generation wireless systems. Shannon Lab uses these for advanced coursework in digital communication and signal processing research projects.

resources.title: WARP Boards
resources.subtitle: Wireless open-access research platform for PHY/MAC prototyping
resources.description: WARP (Wireless Open-Access Research Platform) boards provide a scalable, programmable platform for cutting-edge wireless communication research.
resources.overview: WARP boards are FPGA-based wireless platforms designed for real-time wireless experiments. They support custom PHY and MAC layer implementations, enabling researchers to prototype and test novel wireless algorithms on real hardware. Shannon Lab uses WARP boards for advanced projects involving beamforming, interference management, and cross-layer protocol design. The platform includes reference designs for OFDM, MIMO, and full-duplex communication.

resources.title: DC Communication Trainer Kits
resources.subtitle: Hands-on analog and digital communication training modules
resources.description: Comprehensive trainer kits for teaching modulation, demodulation, encoding, and decoding techniques in both analog and digital domains.
resources.overview: The DC Communication Trainer Kits provide a structured hardware environment for students to explore fundamental communication concepts. Each kit includes pre-built circuit modules for AM, FM, PM, ASK, FSK, PSK, PCM, delta modulation, and line coding schemes. Students can observe real waveforms on oscilloscopes, measure signal quality, and verify theoretical concepts against practical measurements. These kits form the backbone of the Fundamentals of Digital Communication course at Shannon Lab.

resources.title: ZED Boards
resources.subtitle: Xilinx Zynq-7000 SoC development and prototyping board
resources.description: ZedBoard features the Xilinx Zynq-7000 AP SoC, combining a dual-core ARM Cortex-A9 processor with Artix-7 FPGA logic for hardware-software co-design.
resources.overview: The ZedBoard is an evaluation and development board built around the Zynq-7020 AP SoC. It provides a powerful platform for embedded system design, combining the flexibility of FPGA fabric with the processing power of ARM cores. In Shannon Lab, ZedBoards are used for digital signal processing implementations, software-defined radio baseband processing, and hardware acceleration of communication algorithms. Students learn hardware-software co-design methodologies using Xilinx Vivado and the Vitis development environment.

specs.val: HDMI, VGA, USB OTG, Ethernet, OLED

resources.title: Zynq Boards
resources.subtitle: Advanced Zynq SoC platforms for system-level prototyping
resources.description: Zynq-based development boards for implementing complex systems combining programmable logic with high-performance processing.
resources.overview: The Zynq development boards in Shannon Lab leverage the full Zynq-7000 architecture for system-level prototyping. These boards are used for advanced projects requiring tight integration between custom hardware accelerators and software processing - including real-time video processing, high-throughput communication systems, and sensor fusion applications. The boards support high-speed connectivity and are paired with Xilinx development tools for comprehensive FPGA design workflows.

specs.val: GigE, USB, SPI, I2C, UART, GPIO

resources.title: Power Amplifier Module
resources.subtitle: Broadband RF power amplifier for wireless transmission
resources.description: The power amplifier module provides signal amplification for wireless transmission experiments, supporting various modulation schemes and frequency bands.
resources.overview: The Power Amplifier module in Shannon Lab is used for amplifying RF signals in wireless transmission chains. It is essential for experiments involving link budget analysis, over-the-air communication, and antenna testing. Students learn about amplifier linearity, gain compression, and efficiency tradeoffs that are critical in real-world wireless system design. The module supports multi-standard operation and can be cascaded for higher output power applications.
applications: RF link budget and path loss experiments

resources.title: Dual & Tri-Band Antennas
resources.subtitle: Multi-band antennas for wireless communication experiments
resources.description: A collection of dual-band and tri-band antennas supporting 2.4 GHz, 5 GHz, and sub-6 GHz operation for research and teaching.
resources.overview: Shannon Lab maintains a range of dual and tri-band antennas designed for wireless communication experiments. These antennas cover ISM band frequencies (2.4 GHz and 5 GHz) as well as cellular bands, providing the radiating elements needed for over-the-air testing with USRP and WARP platforms. Students use these antennas for antenna pattern measurement, polarization studies, gain characterization, and MIMO spatial multiplexing experiments.

resources.title: NI LabVIEW
resources.subtitle: Graphical programming environment for test and measurement
resources.description: NI LabVIEW provides a graphical development environment for designing measurement, test, and control systems with built-in USRP integration.
resources.overview: NI LabVIEW is the primary software platform used in Shannon Lab for instrument control and signal processing. Its graphical dataflow programming paradigm enables students to rapidly prototype communication systems - from basic modulation schemes to complex MIMO receivers. LabVIEW seamlessly integrates with NI USRP hardware for real-time RF signal acquisition and generation. The Communications Design Suite adds specialized toolkits for LTE, Wi-Fi, and 5G NR waveform generation and analysis.
applications: USRP hardware control and signal acquisition

resources.title: Keysight SystemVue
resources.subtitle: Electronic system-level design environment for communication
resources.description: SystemVue is a focused EDA environment for electronic system-level design, enabling simulation and verification of communication systems and signal processing algorithms.
resources.overview: Keysight SystemVue provides a simulation-centric environment for designing and verifying communication signal processing systems. It bridges the gap between algorithm design and hardware implementation with support for C++, MATLAB, and HDL co-simulation. Shannon Lab uses SystemVue for link-level simulation of wireless standards, BER analysis, and system architecture exploration before deploying algorithms on FPGA or SDR hardware.

resources.title: OptiSystem v14
resources.subtitle: Optical communication system simulation software
resources.description: OptiSystem enables the design, simulation, and optimization of virtually any type of optical link in the physical layer of a broad spectrum of optical networks.
resources.overview: OptiSystem version 14 by Optiwave is used in Shannon Lab for simulating fiber-optic communication systems. It provides a comprehensive environment for designing WDM networks, analyzing link performance, and exploring advanced modulation formats for optical communication. Students use OptiSystem to understand the interplay between fiber dispersion, nonlinear effects, and amplifier noise in long-haul and metro optical networks.

projects.title: Touchless Tap
projects.description: ECE lab developed a cost-effective product to ensure safety during the Corona pandemic. The sensor-based water tap works without manual operation when it detects your hand under the nozzle. Designed and developed by Research Engineers Sana & Khagendra.
